parts.title: Technology Teacher
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] Technology class that meets the Lgr 22 goals for [specific focus, e.g. technical solutions], including an introduction and three activities.
Give me five strategies for motivating a [grade X] student who finds Technology difficult, with one simple and practical idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class a basic Technology skill (e.g. [specific skill, e.g. building a bridge]), with three steps and one example.
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. sustainable development] into a Technology lesson for a [grade X] class, with one project for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases to build a [grade X] student's confidence during a Technology project.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a Technology lesson with a [grade X] class, with three steps and a short problem-solving task.
Suggest three ways to use everyday materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. cardboard]) in a Technology lesson for a [grade X] class, with one building project for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute closing routine for a Technology lesson with a [grade X] class, covering three elements (e.g. reflection, presentation).
Give me five ideas for making a Technology lesson more playful for a [grade X] class, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. construction], with one activity for each.
Create a list of three short reflection questions for a [grade X] class after a Technology lesson on [specific theme, e.g. energy], to capture their insights.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with Science teachers at a [lower secondary school] to connect Technology with [specific theme, e.g. physics], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's technical skills in [specific task, e.g. building a model], with three criteria.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. apps, videos) for a Technology lesson with a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. the design process].
Suggest three ways to adapt a Technology lesson for a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. motor difficulties], with one solution for each.
Create a weekly planning template for Technology for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. construction, problem-solving, reflection).
Give me five ideas for using minimal equipment in a Technology lesson with a [grade X] class to explore [specific theme, e.g. mechanics], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific technical concept, e.g. levers], with one hands-on activity for each.
Write a short guide for managing a disruptive Technology lesson with a [grade X] class, with three steps and one calming strategy.
Give me an example of a Technology lesson for a [grade X] class that combines Technology with [specific theme, e.g. the environment], with three elements.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with Art teachers at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. design], with one example for each.
Create a lesson for a [grade X] class in which they build a simple model to solve [specific problem, e.g. water storage], with three steps and one goal.
Give me five ways to make a theoretical Technology lesson more engaging for a [grade X] class, with one creative idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class to design a [specific structure, e.g. a catapult], with three steps and one testing stage.
Suggest three ways to connect a Technology lesson for a [grade X] class to [specific theme, e.g. digital technology], with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short hands-on activities for a [grade X] Technology class that strengthen [specific goal, e.g. technical analysis], with a description for each.
Give me an example of a closing routine for a Technology lesson with a [grade X] class, with three steps.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools (e.g. [specific tool, e.g. Tinkercad]) in a Technology lesson for a [grade X] class, with one task for each.
Write a short template for a 5-minute reflection after a Technology lesson with a [grade X] class, with three questions about their technical solutions.
Give me five ideas for incorporating play into a Technology lesson with a [grade X] class, focusing on [specific goal, e.g. strength and durability], with one game for each.
Create a list of three short ways to support a [grade X] student who is struggling with [specific concept, e.g. circuits], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with Mathematics teachers at a [compulsory school] to connect Technology with [specific theme, e.g. calculations], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a [grade X] class to build a simple machine within one lesson, with three steps.
Write a list of five simple Technology projects for a [grade X] class that strengthen [specific goal, e.g. innovation], with a brief rationale for each.
Suggest three ways to manage a [grade X] class that loses focus during a Technology lesson, with one method for each.
Create a term planning template for Technology for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. construction, theory, sustainability) and time frames.
Give me five ideas for creating a simple Technology exhibition with a [grade X] class, with minimal preparation, and one element for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific technical concept, e.g. programming], with one activity for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. concentration difficulties] in a Technology lesson, with three steps.
Give me an example of a lesson for a [grade X] class that combines Technology with [specific theme, e.g. space exploration], with three elements.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the student council at a [compulsory school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. technological innovations], with one example for each.
